data.title: Anthropic expands AI access restrictions to China-controlled firms over security concerns
data.description:
AI startup Anthropic is restricting access to its services for companies controlled by China, citing national security risks.

The San Francisco-based startup is widening existing restrictions on “authoritarian” regimes to cover any company that’s majority-owned by entities from countries such as China. That includes their overseas operations, it said in a statement.

"When these entities access our services through subsidiaries, they could use our capabilities to develop applications and services that ultimately serve adversarial military and intelligence services and broader authoritarian objectives," it said.

They could also potentially use the models to advance their own AI development through techniques like distillation and to compete globally with "trusted technology companies headquartered in the United States and allied countries."

The update prohibits companies or organizations whose ownership structures subject them to control from jurisdictions where the startup's products are not permitted, like China, regardless of where they operate.

Anthropic, which is known for its large language model Claude, has significant investment commitments from Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL). It is also seen as a rival to OpenAI, which has major backing from Microsoft (MSFT).

data.title: Newmark secures $425M refinancing for national self-storage portfolio
data.description:
• Newmark Group (NASDAQ:NMRK) said on Thursday that it has arranged a $425M loan on behalf of Centerbridge Partners and Merit Hill Capital for the refinancing of a national portfolio of 78 self-storage properties. 
  • Newmark Co-President of Global Debt & Structured Finance Jordan Roeschlaub, Vice Chairman Nick Scribani and Director John Caraviello secured the financing from Citigroup and Goldman Sachs.
  • The portfolio totals nearly 32,000 units across 4.65M rentable square feet and is strategically located in multiple states.

data.title: Rivian lays off 1.5% workers as it prepares for cheaper R2 SUV
data.description:
Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) made its second small workforce reduction of the year, cutting around 150 employee, less than 1.5% of its total staff, as it prepares for the launch of its more affordable R2 SUV in 2026, the Wall Street Journal reported.

The latest reduction primarily targets Rivian’s commercial teams, which include sales and service roles.

“We have made some recent changes to the commercial team as part of an ongoing effort to improve operational efficiency for R2,” a Rivian spokesperson said, as per the report. The commercial team handles sales and servicing operations for the company. 

The company previously cut about 140 employees from its manufacturing team in June 2025 as part of ongoing efficiency measures.

These actions align with Rivian’s need to reduce per-vehicle costs, streamline operations, and address mounting cost pressures in a competitive electric vehicle market.

Automakers also brace for the expiration of the $7,500 U.S. federal EV tax credit at the end of September, which is expected to impact production volumes and market demand.

data.title: France trade balance improves in July
data.description:
In July 2025, France's trade balance improved (+€0.2 billion) to -€6.5 billion.Over the month, imports increased by €0.3 billion to reach €57.7 billion. Exports increased by €0.5 billion to €51.2 billion.The energy balance improved (+€0.3 billion) due to an increase in exports and a decrease in imports. The non-energy balance deteriorated slightly (-€0.1 billion).

data.title: Prime Drink scraps rights offering, plans to raise $5M in private placement unit offering
Goldman Sachs BDC prices public offering of $400 million of 5.650% unsecured notes due 2030
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs BDC (NYSE:GSBD) has priced an offering of $400 million aggregate principal amount of 5.650% notes due 2030.
  • The 2030 notes will mature on September 9, 2030, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at the company's option at any time at par plus a “make-whole” premium, if applicable.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of this offering to pay down debt under its revolving credit facility and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is subject to customary closing conditions, and the 2030 notes are expected to be delivered on or about September 9.

data.title: Bombardier prices additional debt offering of $250M via issuance of senior notes
data.description:
Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) has announced the pricing of a $250 million offering of senior notes due in 2033. These new notes are an addition to the company's existing $500 million series issued in May, creating a single series with a total value of $750 million.

The new notes will pay a 6.750% annual interest rate and were sold at a price of 103.5% of their principal amount, plus accrued interest since May 29, 2025.

The issuance of the additional notes is expected to close on or about September 18, 2025.

Bombardier plans to use the money from this new offering, along with its existing cash, to pay off some of its outstanding debt, which includes redeeming all of its remaining $166.3 million in 7.125% senior notes due in 2026, to redeem approximately $84 million of its 7.875% senior notes due in 2027.

data.title: Chinese solar stocks surge amid hopes of industry rebound
data.description:
Chinese solar stocks extended their rally on Friday, buoyed by growing optimism that the sector may be nearing a turning point.

Investor sentiment received a boost from government efforts to tackle overcapacity and bring an end to a bruising price war.

The CSI Photovoltaic Industry Index climbed as much as 5.9%, putting it on pace for its strongest weekly gain since October of last year. Leading the charge were Sungrow Power Supply Co. and CSI Solar Co., each posting gains of over 10%.

In the latest indication of improving industry conditions, wafer prices in China extended gains this week and rose between 0.7% and 2.4%, as per the latest data from the China Silicon Industry Association.

“Overseas demand remains strong and foreign buyers have relatively high tolerance for wafer price increases,” the association said in a note dated Thursday, as per Bloomberg.

Daqo New Energy (NYSE:DQ), a major producer in the polysilicon sector, said Thursday the industry is at a clear turning point.

More broadly, Chinese battery stocks also have rallied recently, supported by robust energy storage demand. Eve Energy Co.’s shares have jumped 25% this week, making the company one of the best performers in the MSCI Asia Pacific Index, the report said.

data.title: UK retail sales rises 1.1% Y/Y in July
data.description:
Retail sales in the UK increased 0.60% in July of 2025 over the previous month. 

Y/Y retail sales increased 1.1% in July.

data.title: Trump to impose tariffs on semiconductor imports from companies not moving production to the US
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday that his administration would impose tariffs on semiconductor imports from companies not shifting production to the U.S.

“Yeah, I have discussed it with the people here. Chips and semiconductors—we will be putting tariffs on companies that aren't coming in. We will be putting a tariff very shortly,” Trump told reporters. 

“We will be putting a very substantial tariff, not that high, but a fairly substantial tariff, with the understanding that if they come into the country, if they are coming in, building, or planning to come in, there will not be a tariff,” Trump said. 

“Like, I would say Tim Cook would be in pretty good shape,” he added. 

Apple (AAPL) recently raised its total domestic investment commitment in the U.S. by $100B to $600B over the next four years to avoid tariffs on its iPhones.
